title: d_s^UV(z, D, probe) classification table (reframed hypothesis L3-1)

formalism.return_probability: P(sigma) = INT d^D k exp(-sigma F(k))
formalism.spectral_dimension: d_s(sigma) = -2 d ln P / d ln sigma
formalism.isotropic_master_UV: d_s^UV = D / gamma  where F(k) ~ k^{2 gamma} (UV)
formalism.anisotropic_horava: d_s = 1 + D_space / z  (time power 1, space power z)

conventions_validated.CST_dAlembertian_Belenchia_1507.00330: universal UV d_s -> 2 in all D

classification_table.source: standard QFT heat kernel; Carlip 1705.05417 review
classification_table.note: F=k^2, gamma=1, d_s=D=4 at all scales (no flow)
UV F~k^4, gamma=2, d_s=D/2=2; IR F~k^2, d_s=4
eta_*=2-d=-2 -> propagator 1/p^4 -> UV d_s=d/2=2; IR d_s=4
UNIVERSAL UV d_s=2 in all D (non-local BD operator); IR d_s=D
UV d_s INCREASES above D (Lorentzian non-locality); IR d_s=D. Opposite UV trend to the d'Alembertian probe -- SAME theory, different probe.
canonical UV multifractional pick d_s->2; IR d_s=D

reproduced_from_literature: Asymptotic Safety UV d_s=2 from eta=-2 -- hep-th/0508202, 1110.5224
Causal set d'Alembertian universal UV d_s=2 -- arXiv:1507.00330
Causal set random-walk increasing UV d_s -- arXiv:1311.2530 (qualitative)
our_contribution: ONE return-probability formalism P(sigma)=INT d^Dk e^{-sigma F(k)} applied NUMERICALLY and uniformly across GR/Horava/Stelle/AS/causal-sets/multifractional.

PROBE introduced as an explicit THIRD classification axis: the SAME theory (causal sets) yields OPPOSITE UV trends (d'Alembertian: d_s->2 vs random walk: d_s increases). This makes (z, D, probe) the classifier, not (z, D) alone.
Reframing the apparent 'd_s->2 convergence' as a CLASSIFICATION/discriminator: d_s^UV is a fingerprint of (UV propagator exponent, dimension, probe), NOT a universal constant.
